*Boswell audit — BannermanLeasing — 2026-05-23 05:10*

# BannermanLeasing / Bannerman Content Machine — Handoff Document

---

## 1. Technical Handoff

### Architecture

```
BannermanLeasing/
│
├── index.html                        # Vite entry point
├── src/
│   └── main.jsx                      # React DOM bootstrap (mounts App)
│
├── App.jsx                           # Main React component, routing, sidebar dashboard
├── bannerman-cm-api.js               # Frontend: calls /api/llm/generate, parses LLM output
├── bannerman-cm-queue.js             # 365-day content calendar builder, sessionStorage state
├── bannerman-cm-config.js            # 52 weeks of topics + Bannerman brand voice (hardcoded)
├── bannerman-cm-content-display.jsx  # Per-platform content renderer (blog, IG, TikTok, etc.)
├── bannerman-cm-supabase.sql         # DB schema: cm_tasks table, state machine DDL
│
├── types/
│   └── index.d.ts                    # QueueTask / ContentTask TypeScript types (JSDoc bridge)
│
├── functions/                        # Cloudflare Pages Functions (server-side Workers)
│   └── api/
│       ├── _cors.js                  # Shared CORS middleware
│       ├── health.js                 # GET /api/health — reports which LLM keys are set
│       ├── llm/
│       │   ├── generate.js           # POST /api/llm/generate — validates, rate-limits
│       │   └── call.js               # Routes to Anthropic / OpenAI / Google, normalizes response
│       ├── anthropic/
│       │   └── index.js              # Legacy/direct Anthropic proxy (separate from llm/)
│       └── automation/
│           ├── seed.js               # POST /api/automation/seed — populates cm_tasks in Supabase
│           ├── run-daily.js          # POST /api/automation/run-daily — fetches + generates due tasks
│           ├── retry-failed.js       # POST /api/automation/retry-failed — re-runs failed tasks
│           ├── export.js             # GET  /api/automation/export — JSON or CSV dump
│           └── status.js             # GET  /api/automation/status — queue health metrics
│
├── e2e/                              # Playwright end-to-end tests
├── vite.config.js                    # Vite build config (output: dist/)
├── wrangler.toml                     # Cloudflare Pages project config
└── package.json

DATA FLOW

Browser (React SPA)
  │
  │  1. Builds 365-day queue in memory from bannerman-cm-config.js
  │  2. Persists queue state to sessionStorage (volatile — lost on tab close)
  │  3. User clicks Generate
  │
  ▼
POST /api/llm/generate   (Cloudflare Pages Function — server-side)
  │  - Validates prompt size, token limit
  │  - Checks rate limit via KV namespace
  │  - Routes to selected LLM provider
  │
  ├──► Anthropic Claude API
  ├──► OpenAI GPT-4 API
  └──► Google Gemini API
        │
        └─► Normalized { content: [{ text }] } response
              │
              ▼
         Browser parses ---TAG--- delimiters
         into platform-specific content objects
         (blog metadata/markdown, carousel slides, TikTok scripts, etc.)

OPTIONAL AUTONOMOUS PATH (requires Supabase + GitHub Actions cron)

GitHub Actions cron
  │
  ▼
POST /api/automation/run-daily
  │
  ├──► Supabase (cm_tasks table)  ← seed.js populates this once
  │     reads pending tasks due today
  │
  ├──► LLM API (server-side, same providers)
  │
  └──► Supabase (updates rows: pending → ready | failed)
        │
        └──► Optional webhook (CM_WEBHOOK_URL) with run summary

EXTERNAL SERVICES
  Anthropic API      (content generation)
  OpenAI API         (content generation, alternative)
  Google AI API      (content generation, alternative)
  Supabase           (autonomous mode only — task persistence)
  Cloudflare Pages   (hosting + Workers runtime for functions/)
  Cloudflare KV      (rate limiting — must be bound as RATE_LIMIT_KV)
```

---

### Build & Deploy

**Local development:**
```bash
git clone <repo-url>
cd bannerman-content-machine
npm install
cp .dev.vars.example .dev.vars
# Edit .dev.vars — set at least one of:
#   ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY
npm run dev
# Vite dev server starts; Cloudflare functions run via wrangler pages dev
```

**Production build:**
```bash
npm run build
# Runs: vite build && cp -R functions dist/ && cp bannerman-cm-config.js dist/
# Output: dist/ (static assets + functions/ subdirectory + config)
```

**Deploy to Cloudflare Pages:**
```bash
npx wrangler pages deploy dist --project-name bannerman-content-machine
```

Then in the Cloudflare Pages dashboard → Settings → Environment Variables (Production), set:
- At minimum: one of `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `GOOGLE_API_KEY`
- For automation: `SUPABASE_URL`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, `CM_AUTOMATION_KEY`
- For rate limiting: create a KV namespace and bind it as `RATE_LIMIT_KV`
- Optional: `BANNERMAN_CLIENT_TOKEN`, `CM_LLM_PROVIDER`, `CM_LLM_MODEL`, `CM_WEBHOOK_URL`, `ALLOWED_ORIGIN`, `CM_DAILY_BATCH_SIZE`

**Run tests:**
```bash
npm test              # Vitest unit tests
npm run test:e2e      # Playwright (requires dev server running)
```

**What works:**
- Vite build pipeline is clean and straightforward
- Cloudflare Pages deployment path is documented and functional
- LLM proxy correctly handles all three providers
- CORS middleware is consistently applied
- Health endpoint works

**What is known to be incomplete or risky:**
- `.dev.vars.example` is referenced in README but not confirmed present in repo — the file may need to be created manually before local dev works
- Autonomous mode requires a separate manual Supabase setup step (run `.sql` file, configure GitHub Actions cron) — none of this is automated or tested in CI
- KV namespace binding for rate limiting must be done manually in the Cloudflare dashboard; there is no `wrangler.toml` binding declaration for it, so it silently fails to rate-limit if not configured
- The build script uses `cp -R` which will fail on Windows without WSL or Git Bash

**CI/CD story:**
There is none currently. No GitHub Actions workflow file exists for automated builds or deployments. The autonomous mode mentions GitHub Actions cron for `run-daily` but no workflow file is included. A developer would need to create this from scratch.

---

### The 5 Most Important Files

1. **`functions/api/llm/generate.js`** — The security and access control boundary for all LLM calls; handles rate limiting, prompt size enforcement, token caps, and client token auth. If this is wrong, API keys are exposed or abusable.

2. **`bannerman-cm-config.js`** — Contains the entire 52-week topic schedule and Bannerman Leasing brand voice; this is the only place the product's editorial identity lives, and it is the first thing a new owner would need to customize.

3. **`bannerman-cm-queue.js`** — Builds the 365-day content calendar and manages all queue state; understanding this file is required before touching any other frontend feature.

4. **`functions/api/automation/run-daily.js`** — The core of the autonomous content pipeline; fetches due tasks from Supabase, calls the LLM, and writes results back — this is what makes the system "autonomous" rather than manual.

5. **`bannerman-cm-supabase.sql`** — Defines the `cm_tasks` database schema and state machine; without running this, the entire autonomous mode is non-functional and there is no persistent task storage.

---

### What's Broken Right Now

- **State is lost on every tab close.** Queue state is stored in `sessionStorage` only. Refreshing or closing the tab destroys all generated content and queue progress. There is no localStorage fallback, no server-side save for interactive mode.

- **Rate limiting silently does nothing if KV is not bound.** The `RATE_LIMIT_KV` binding is not declared in `wrangler.toml`. If the namespace isn't manually bound in the Cloudflare dashboard, rate limiting code will error or silently skip, with no warning to the operator.

- **KV rate-limit implementation has a memory leak vector.** Timestamps are stored as a JSON array that grows with each request and is only trimmed by a sliding window check. Under sustained load this array grows unboundedly per IP/token key.

- **`generateContentForTask` is duplicated between `run-daily.js` and `retry-failed.js`** with hardcoded LLM prompts in both. Any prompt change must be made in two places; they can silently diverge.

- **`functions/api/anthropic/index.js` is a legacy endpoint** that duplicates functionality now handled by `functions/api/llm/generate.js`. It has minimal error handling — non-200 upstream responses pass through silently with no logging or client-friendly message.

- **Build script (`cp -R`) breaks on Windows** natively. No cross-platform copy utility (e.g., `copyfiles` or `vite-plugin-static-copy`) is used.

- **No GitHub Actions workflow file exists** despite the README documenting a cron-based autonomous mode that depends on one.

---

### What's Half-Built

- **Autonomous mode (Supabase + cron):** The server-side code exists and appears functional, but the GitHub Actions cron workflow that would actually trigger it daily is not in the repository. The operator has to create and configure this manually. Without it, `run-daily.js` is never called automatically.

- **Authentication:** There is an optional `BANNERMAN_CLIENT_TOKEN` / `x-bannerman-token` header mechanism, but it's opt-in and off by default. The app runs fully open (no auth) unless the operator explicitly sets the env var. There is no user login, no session management, no multi-tenant separation.

- **TypeScript / type safety:** `types/index.d.ts` defines `QueueTask` and `ContentTask`, but the codebase is entirely `.js` and `.jsx` with JSDoc. TypeScript checking is not enforced in the build or CI. `QueueTask.content` is typed as `Record<string, unknown>`, meaning platform-specific content shapes are unvalidated at runtime.

- **Export functionality (interactive mode):** Export is implemented server-side for autonomous/Supabase tasks (`/api/automation/export`), but it's unclear whether the React frontend has a working export UI that uses it, or whether export from the browser-only queue (sessionStorage) is implemented.

- **Webhook support:** `CM_WEBHOOK_URL` is documented and wired into `run-daily.js`, but there is no retry logic, signature/authentication on the webhook payload, or error handling if the webhook endpoint is down.

- **Testing coverage:** Vitest and Playwright are configured and there are e2e tests for basic UI flows with mocked API responses, but there appear to be no unit tests for the queue builder, config parser, LLM output parser, or automation endpoints — the most complex and failure-prone code.

---

### Next 3 Things a Developer Should Do

1. **Replace sessionStorage with a durable client-side store (localStorage + optional Supabase sync).** This is the single highest-impact fix: right now every user loses all work on tab close. Swap `sessionStorage` for `localStorage` in `bannerman-cm-queue.js` as a minimum viable fix (one-line change per key), then optionally wire the interactive queue to the same Supabase `cm_tasks` table already used by automation. This unblocks any real usage of the product.

2. **Add the GitHub Actions workflow file for the daily cron.** Create `.github/workflows/daily-run.yml` that runs on schedule (`0 8 * * *`), calls `POST /api/automation/run-daily` with the `x-cm-automation-key` header, and logs the result. Without this, the "autonomous mode" described throughout the README is entirely manual. This is a ~20-line YAML file that makes the product's main differentiator actually work.

3. **Extract `generateContentForTask` into a shared module and add a `wrangler.toml` KV binding declaration.** Deduplicating the function from `run-daily.js` and `retry-failed.js` into `functions/api/_generate-content.js` eliminates the silent prompt-divergence bug. Adding `[[kv_namespaces]]` to `wrangler.toml` makes rate limiting actually enforced in deployed environments and removes the silent-failure footgun. Both are low-risk refactors with high operational impact.

---

## 2. Business / Product Handoff

**What this app does:** Bannerman Content Machine is a web app that generates and schedules 365 days of social media and blog content for a property leasing company using AI, with optional fully autonomous daily generation via Supabase and a cron job.

**Who it's for:** A single property management or leasing company (specifically Bannerman Leasing) — a small business operator or their marketing person who needs consistent multi-platform content (blog, Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, Twitter) without hiring a content team.

**Problem it solves:** Small leasing companies have no budget for a content agency but need consistent, on-brand publishing across 5 platforms to stay visible to landlords and tenants. Manually writing 365 days of content per platform is months of work. This app generates a full year's worth of platform-specific content from a single topic schedule using LLMs, with brand voice and topic themes baked in. The autonomous mode means the owner can theoretically set it up once and have content ready to post every day without logging in.

**Estimated value:** Low-to-moderate as a product; moderate as a template. As a bespoke internal tool for Bannerman Leasing specifically, it has limited resale value without rebranding — the topics, voice, and queue are hardcoded to landlord/leasing themes and Bannerman's identity. As a white-label "content machine for small businesses" template, it's worth $2,000–$8,000 to a developer or agency who wants a working starting point. It is not SaaS-ready (no multi-tenancy, no billing, no user accounts). A buyer would essentially be purchasing the architecture and the solved problems (multi-LLM proxy, Cloudflare Pages deployment, Supabase automation pattern), not a customer base.

**Customers:** No evidence of any real users or paying customers. This appears to be a tool built for the founder's own business (Bannerman Leasing) or as a proof-of-concept. There is no user authentication, no billing integration, and no analytics instrumentation.

**Unit economics:**
- **LLM cost per generation:** Roughly $0.01–$0.05 per content piece depending on provider and platform (blog posts cost more tokens than tweets). A full 365-day seed run would cost approximately $5–$20 in API fees depending on provider.
- **Hosting:** Cloudflare Pages free tier handles this comfortably at low traffic. KV and Workers usage would stay within free tier limits for a single-company tool.
- **Revenue:** None detected. No Stripe, no subscriptions, no payment infrastructure.

**Recommended fate:** **Open-source or Sell as a template.**

The tool is well-architected for its scope and solves a real problem, but it is too business-specific (Bannerman brand voice, leasing topics) and too incomplete (no auth, volatile state, no CI) to operate as a product without significant work. Open-sourcing it as a "content machine template for Cloudflare Pages + Supabase + multi-LLM" would generate goodwill and potentially attract contributors. Alternatively, sell it as a developer template for $500–$2,500 on a marketplace like Gumroad or Lemon Squeezy after fixing the sessionStorage and CI gaps. Sunsetting would waste the solid architectural work already done.

## 3. Estate / Access Handoff

**Domains:** No custom domain is configured in the codebase. The
